runs.answer: Bacteria have been using CRISPR-Cas9 to defend themselves against viruses for millions of years. When a bacterium encounters a virus, a fragment of the virus's genetic material is incorporated into the bacterium's genome. This sequence is then transcribed into RNA, which is used by the Cas9 enzyme
